title: Karnali Province - The Kathmandu Post
authors: By Krishna Prasad Gautam & Tularam Pandey
summary: Bajhang phone users battle patchy network By Basanta Pratap Singh Users complain of weak connectivity, call drops, and one-way audio. Death toll in Salyan jeep crash rises to four By Biplov Maharjan Victims include three who died on the spot; six among the injured in critical condition. Karnali solar project set to light up Jumla with clean power By DB Budha Around 1 MW plant under construction in
content:
Bajhang phone users battle patchy network By Basanta Pratap Singh Users complain of weak connectivity, call drops, and one-way audio. Death toll in Salyan jeep crash rises to four By Biplov Maharjan Victims include three who died on the spot; six among the injured in critical condition. Karnali solar project set to light up Jumla with clean power By DB Budha Around 1 MW plant under construction in Khalanga aims to serve 9,000 consumers. Bheri Babai diversion project civil works miss third deadline, only 57 percent complete By Krishna Prasad Gautam Contractor seeks another extension as delays pile up from design changes to material shortages. Question paper leaked minutes after exam begins in Karnali By DB Budha Authorities launch investigation after papers circulate online during SEE exams; officials say action will be taken against those responsible. Bheri bridge project stalls for years, leaving key highway link incomplete By Krishna Prasad Gautam Failed contracts and rejected bids keep Rukum West–Jajarkot connection stranded as locals rely on suspension bridge. Karnali students haven’t been taught in full, yet they will sit for SEE next week By Krishna Prasad Gautam & Tularam Pandey Some opt for private tuition away from their villages paying high costs. Family refuses to claim body, demands impartial probe into Inisa BK death case By Jyotee Katuwal 16-year-old’s body remains at Karnali Province Hospital for 18 days as police say investigation in final stages. Vote counting suspended in Rukum West By Post Report Counting halted following votes mismatch. Congress candidate Basana Thapa takes early lead in Dailekh-1 By Jyotee Katuwal UML’s Rabindraraj Sharma follows as vote counting continues. RPP’s Gyan Bahadur Shahi takes early lead in Jumla By DB Budha Shahi ahead after first 400 votes counted; NCP candidate Naresh Bhandari follows. Suspicious item neutralised, voting resumes in Jumla By DB Budha Army neutralises suspicious object, polling resumes safely. Voting begins at most centres in Dolpa despite cold By Post Report Extreme cold delays voting at some polling centres in Shey Phoksundo Rural Municipality. Two election staff airlifted from upper Dolpa polling centre By Ram Chandra Neupane Officials develop breathing complications at high-altitude site in ward 6 of Shey Phoksundo Rural Municipality. Nepali Army to conduct aerial patrols in sensitive polling centres in Karnali By Jyotee Katuwal 356 highly sensitive sites under north western division; air surveillance begins ahead of March 5 vote. Upper Dolpa faces delays in delivering election materials to 12 polling centres By Ram Chandra Neupane Helicopters grounded by strong winds; authorities may deliver election materials on foot. Rukum West lists 21 polling centres as highly sensitive By Mahesh KC Security agencies cite remoteness and political rivalry as key concerns; no incidents reported so far. Solve water crisis and get our vote, Rekcha village declares By Tripti Shahi The village in Surkhet has seen more than half of its residents migrate elsewhere over the past five years mainly because of water scarcity. Driver killed, three injured in Salyan jeep crash By Biplab Maharjan Vehicle plunges 400 metres off Rapti Highway in Kapurkot. Election police training begins in Salyan By Biplab Maharjan Over 1,400 personnel, including 447 women, to be deployed across 117 polling stations.
